pQleUreAcLomITmYe &nd SaAtioFnETisYp rAeDseVntICedEin Figure 3.
Etch Rate and Depth Control:
Both recipes start with an estimated etch time based on typical etch rates
for SiO2 in an ICP RIE system. The actual etch time should be adjusted
based on periodic depth measurements to ensure the desired 2 µm depth is
achieved.
Anisotropy and Selectivity:
Recipe 1 uses higher ICP power and a balanced gas mixture to achieve a
good etch rate while maintaining anisotropy and selectivity. Recipe 2 focuses
more on anisotropy by using lower RF power and slightly higher pressure,
which helps achieve more vertical sidewalls.
Uniformity and Damage:
Both recipes aim to minimize physical damage by optimizing the power
settings and gas flows. The use of CHF3 helps in providing sidewall
protection, while Ar and O2 help in maintaining plasma stability and
enhancing etch product volatility, respectively.
Safety Considerations:
Safety procedures are emphasized to ensure safe handling of gases and
operation of the plasma etching system. It is crucial to confirm with the
facility and equipment manager that the planned recipes are compatible with
the system.
